name: HTTP gateway
summary: Small local HTTP gateway for inbound jobs, channels, and session checks.

name: MCP gateway
summary: MCP sidecar for orchestrator/soul-mode sessions. Spawned per session by the http-gateway as a stdio MCP child; hosts the garrison-control tool set (talk_to, wait_for, session and worktree management).

name: Basic Memory
summary: Obsidian-native, plain-markdown memory backed by Basic Memory. Indexes the vault into a local SQLite knowledge graph and exposes write/search/read MCP tools to Claude, Codex, and Gemini. Auto-captures a session checkpoint on SessionEnd/PreCompact.

name: Trello connector
summary: Trello connector — board lists and cards as a Vault-sealed callable action catalog.

name: WhatsApp connector
summary: WhatsApp connector — send text/template messages and list templates on the WhatsApp Business Cloud API as a Vault-sealed callable action catalog.

name: Spotify connector
summary: Spotify connector — control playback (pause/next/play-by-search/volume) on the active Spotify Connect device over the Web API. Needs Premium; OAuth2 sealed in the Vault.

name: Google Workspace connector
summary: Google Workspace connector — Gmail/Drive/Calendar as a Vault-sealed OAuth2 action catalog.

name: Slack channel
summary: Receives Slack app_mention and DM events, round-trips via the gateway's /chat.

name: Web channel
summary: Mobile-first browser chat surface. Serves a React UI on its own port (default 27083) and proxies the http-gateway for streaming replies.

name: Browser
summary: Headless Chromium substrate on port 27084. JPEG screencast, mouse/key/touch input, raw CDP, and Chromium's built-in DevTools reverse-proxied for any browser on the Tailnet.

name: Voice (Deepgram)
summary: Speech I/O on port 27085. Proxies Deepgram speech-to-text and text-to-speech, keeping the API key server-side; provides a kind:voice capability that channels consume for push-to-talk and read-aloud.

name: Voice (Local)
summary: Fully local, multilingual speech I/O on port 7090. No cloud, no API key: a Node wrapper supervises a faster-whisper STT + Kokoro/Piper TTS voice-server and exposes the same kind:voice contract as deepgram-voice. STT auto-detects the spoken language (PT/FR/EN/ES/IT) and TTS replies in that language's own voice — Portuguese via Piper's native European pt_PT voice, the rest via Kokoro — so channels consume it unchanged for push-to-talk and read-aloud.

name: Jarvis Agentic OS
summary: Voice-first Jarvis-style HUD on port 7092. Central audio-reactive core (DitherCore), hold-to-talk → Orchestrator (via the gateway) → reply read aloud. Consumes a kind:voice Fitting. Visual layer reused from the Fable jarvis-hud reference.

name: Dev Env
summary: Consolidated dev surface on port 27086 — every Claude Code session is a tab with a Claude PTY + shell PTY and a live browser pane; worktree lifecycle and session status built in.

name: Screen share
summary: macOS screenshot capture — ~2fps JPEG polling viewer for phone/remote access.

name: Ports
summary: Lists every listening TCP port on this box, labels each from the worktree registry / ui-fitting status files / owning process, and offers open-over-tailnet, open-in-Browser-pane, copy-URL, and guarded kill actions. Serves its own phone-friendly React UI on its own port (default 27088).

name: Outpost
summary: Garrison Outpost bridge for a Tailscale-connected remote Mac. Spawn processes, read files, and manage git worktrees on the remote machine via Garrison Outpost Protocol v1.

name: Monitor (default)
summary: Read-only visibility into every entity Garrison spawns — PIDs, status, ports, logs. Serves its own React UI on its own port (default 27077).

name: Power (default)
summary: Idle watcher that self-suspends the box when nothing is running - evaluates dev-env sessions, kanban cards, presence, SSH, load, and a Keep Awake pin, then suspends the GCE instance after a continuous-clear window. Serves a mobile-first control UI on its own port (default 27092).

name: Orchestrator
summary: Orchestrator Faculty - the single brain. Owns the compiled policy (task types, matrix, phase plans, work kinds, skill bindings), the preRoute (task-type + tier + execution) and the {{routing}} injection; configured from the Muster Orchestrator tab. Absorbs the former Model Router.

name: Improver
summary: Nightly self-improvement runner — reads telemetry, transcripts, and evidence and produces proposal diffs into an own-port review queue (default 27093). Approved proposals apply only through hosted authoring APIs. Per-rule autonomy (manual|auto) with streak-gated promotion and instant demotion.

name: Codex runtime
summary: Runtime Faculty (secondary) — drives `codex exec` behind the uniform RuntimeAdapter + runtime-bridge delegate() contract. The orchestrator routes `secondary:codex` coding tasks here; the primary calls the bridge and Codex does the work in its own loop.

name: Gemini runtime
summary: Runtime Faculty (secondary) — drives `gemini -p` behind the uniform RuntimeAdapter + runtime-bridge delegate() contract. The orchestrator's image/video roles resolve to `secondary:gemini`; the primary calls the bridge and receives the artifact path(s).

name: OpenCode runtime
summary: Runtime Faculty (secondary) - drives `opencode run` (stateless subprocess, prompt via stdin, JSON event stream, `-s` session resume) behind the uniform RuntimeAdapter + runtime-bridge delegate() contract. The orchestrator routes `secondary:opencode` tasks here; the primary calls the bridge and OpenCode does the work in its own loop against any configured provider (hosted `opencode/*` or a local Ollama).

name: Scheduler
summary: Cron-style job scheduler. Machine-global jobs file (~/.garrison/scheduler-jobs.json); add/remove/list/run-now plus tick and daemon modes. Fitting setup hooks register jobs; the io.garrison.scheduler launchd agent runs the daemon.

name: Automations engine
summary: YAML automations with 8 step types, a live SSE run viewer, Router-planned authoring, and Vault-sealed connector steps. Provides automation-runner.

name: File Browser
summary: Mobile-first file browser/viewer/editor scoped to a workspace root (Monaco + Markdown + images). The artifact surface that replaces the artifact-store.

name: Drill
summary: Visual, agent-driven QA. Author a Drill Book (pages, areas, steps, states) against the target app, run it live via the automations engine (vision-first, deterministic once graduated), and graduate passing vision steps into committed Playwright specs. Findings triage into batch fix cards on the normal code pipeline; a blind adversarial pass and a viewport matrix catch regressions and mobile-only bugs the graduated specs alone would miss. Provides duty:drill; consumes automation-runner.

name: Vault Git Sync
summary: Commit + push the Basic Memory Obsidian vault to git on a schedule (default nightly), so memory written by any agent syncs across machines. Pure shell; registered as a scheduler job.

name: Coordination — Agent Mail
summary: Shared local agent-coordination server (mcp_agent_mail): identities, messaging, advisory file leases. Own-port :28765, license-isolated arm’s-length process.

name: Coordination — Planning Gate
summary: Garrison-owned MIT planning-gate MCP: begin_planning/end_planning serialize planning per repo (TTL+heartbeat mutex + read-bundle) + digest/nudge hook + the coord observability CLI.

name: Kanban Loop
summary: Workflow state machine as a Kanban board (automation-runner) with a responsive phone-first board UI (own-port :27089, embedded at /embed/kanban-loop). Full garrison-* pipeline: Backlog → To Do → Discuss → Plan → Implement → Review → Adv Review → Test → Adv Test → Walkthrough → Validate → Done (+ needs-attention). File-per-card (ULID, atomic, per-card lock); agent-lists run their garrison-* skill + send the execute+router prompt through the orchestrator (preRoute, hint honored in both gateway modes); the router output exact-matches a valid next list or the card parks. Per-card runId/runDir; cards link artifacts, never duplicate. Goal-mode prepends /goal; the guard is the per-card iteration cap. The two adversarial lists are cross-model Codex passes. Discuss is James-mode web chat producing a brief.

name: Claude Code runtime
summary: Runtime Faculty (default PRIMARY) — the node-pty engine that drives the real interactive Claude Code TUI (packages/claude-pty). Hosts the Operative's orchestrator loop via the HTTP gateway or the direct PTY operative, and registers as a model-router `runtime: claude-code` target. Provider overrides (anthropic-plan default / ollama-local / deepseek / zai-glm) swap ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL so the same Claude Code engine runs against other providers.

name: Agent SDK runtime
summary: Runtime Faculty — implements the uniform RuntimeAdapter over the Claude Agent SDK (structured request/response, native tool-calls — no PTY, no terminal scraping). THE FENCE default-denies Anthropic billing (launches only behind a non-Anthropic base URL: Ollama / DeepSeek / Z.ai / MiniMax / LLM proxy); THE HARNESS wires the full claude_code preset for coding roles or a lean system string for chat roles. The home for everything reached by base URL.

name: Garrison Skills
summary: The garrison family — the gated-build orchestrator doorway plus every phase verb skill — as a Garrison skill-shape fitting, installed into ~/.claude/skills under APM lockfile ownership.

name: Claude Config
summary: Garrison-managed ~/.claude config surfaces (commands, agents, hooks, templates, mcp.json, settings fragments), imported from the decommissioned claude-share repo. Synced via `garrison config`.

name: Snapshots
summary: Off-site, encrypted restic backups of the Garrison state - ~/.garrison, ~/.claude, and the projects root - to a GCS backend, on a systemd user timer independent of Garrison. Restore is manual: the view prints the exact restic restore command.

name: Taste
summary: Design-taste skill pair (design-taste-frontend v2 + redesign-existing-projects) vendored MIT from Leonxlnx/taste-skill at a pinned commit; plain SKILL.md, runtime-agnostic.

name: Taste (copy)
summary: Design-taste skill pair (design-taste-frontend v2 + redesign-existing-projects) vendored MIT from Leonxlnx/taste-skill at a pinned commit; plain SKILL.md, runtime-agnostic.

name: Garrison Assistant
summary: Answer, Guide, Build — grounded Q&A over docs+Fittings, guided-tour launcher, and an interview that files review-queue proposals (provenance assistant). Never edits artifacts directly.

name: Identity Gary
summary: The operative's resting identity — Gary, the base persona. Conversational and prose-first; knows the user, handles the day, tasks, calendar, and questions, and hands work off to the right duty rather

name: Plan
summary: Provides the `plan` duty and owns the garrison-plan skill. Reproduces Claude Code plan mode autonomously: read-only exploration + design, then a durable plan file. The duty's default level binds the s

name: Implement
summary: Provides the `implement` duty and owns the garrison-implement skill. Explores the relevant code first (vision-first for UI), then writes code to satisfy the acceptance, following existing conventions

name: Review
summary: Provides the `review` duty and owns the garrison-review skill. A same-model code review of the current diff for correctness bugs and quality cleanups (reuse, simplification, efficiency).

name: Test
summary: Provides the `test` duty and owns the garrison-test skill. The objective correctness gate: a committed, re-runnable test (default e2e through the UI + unit tests) plus a clean build/typecheck/lint.

name: Adversarial Test
summary: Provides the `adversarial-test` duty and owns the garrison-adversarial-test skill. A fresh-context session that has seen neither the diff nor the committed test drives the running app through the acce

name: Walkthrough
summary: Provides the `walkthrough` duty and owns the garrison-walkthrough skill. Records self-verified video evidence of the change via the walkthrough skill, then surfaces the scrubbable link.

name: Discuss
summary: Provides the `discuss` duty and owns the duty-discuss skill. Think a problem through out loud like a calm whiteboard conversation; when the thinking settles into something reusable, write a brief to d

name: Develop
summary: Provides the `develop` duty and owns the duty-develop skill — the dev face. Develop a change end to end: level 1 is a quick fix (implement only), level 2 plans, implements, reviews, and tests. A compo

name: Video
summary: Produce a video walkthrough of a change by driving the existing walkthrough recorder (narrated, captioned MP4 via playwright-cli capture + ffmpeg stitch + vision self-check). This duty adds no pipelin

name: Image
summary: Produce an image from a prompt via the simplest path that already works in the composition: invoke an image-capable model the operative can reach (the gemini runtime, or an image MCP tool) with the pr

name: Dispatcher
summary: The routing brain, duties-and-levels edition. One single-shot STRUCTURED garrison-call on a small fast model turns a message + the composition's selected duties/levels into a (duty, level) + confidenc

name: Garrison Call
summary: Single-shot / structured LLM calls only — no tool loop, no session, never a primary. Three wire shapes: Anthropic Messages (POST /v1/messages), OpenAI-compatible Chat (POST /v1/chat/completions), and

name: Openai Agents Runtime
summary: A Runtime fitting over OpenAI-compatible endpoints. Implements the RuntimeAdapter contract over the OpenAI Agents SDK (@openai/agents - structured request/response, native function-tool handling, NO P
